name: swh-counters

metadata.name: swh.counters
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: GNU General Public License v3 (GPLv3)

documentation:
swh-counters
============

Service providing efficient estimates of the number of objects in the SWH archive, using
Redis's Hyperloglog

functions.name: swh.counters.get_counters
documentation:
Get an counters object of class `cls` with arguments `args`.

Args:
    cls: counters's class, either 'local' or 'remote'
    args: dictionary of arguments passed to the
        counters class constructor

Returns:
    an instance of swh.counters's classes (either local or remote)

Raises:
    ValueError if passed an unknown counters class.

functions.name: swh.counters.get_history
documentation:
Get a history object of class `cls` with arguments `kwargs`.

Args:
    cls: history's class, only 'prometheus' is supported actually
    kwargs: dictionary of arguments passed to the
        counters class constructor

Returns:
    an instance of swh.counters.history's classes (either local or remote)

Raises:
    ValueError if passed an unknown history class.

functions.name: swh.counters.tests.test_server.write_config_file
documentation:
Prepare configuration file in `$tmpdir/name` with content `content`.

Args:
    tmpdir (LocalPath): root directory
    content: Content of the file either as string or as a dict.
                        If a dict, converts the dict into a yaml string.
    name: configuration filename

Returns
    path of the configuration file prepared.

functions.name: swh.counters.api.server.make_app
documentation: Initialize the remote api application.

functions.name: swh.counters.api.server.load_and_check_config
documentation:
Check the minimal configuration is set to run the api or raise an
   error explanation.

Args:
    config_file: Path to the configuration file to load
    type: configuration type. For 'local' type, more
                checks are done.

Raises:
    Error if the setup is not as expected

Returns:
    configuration as a dict

functions.name: swh.counters.api.server.make_app_from_configfile
documentation:
Run the WSGI app from the webserver, loading the configuration from
a configuration file.

SWH_CONFIG_FILENAME environment variable defines the
configuration path to load.

functions.name: swh.counters.api.server.get_metrics
documentation:
expose the counters values in a prometheus format

detailed format:
# HELP swh_archive_object_total Software Heritage Archive object counters
# TYPE swh_archive_object_total gauge
swh_archive_object_total{col="value",object_type="<collection>"} <value>
...

classes.name: swh.counters.history.History

methods.name: swh.counters.history.History._compute_url
documentation:
Compute the api url to request data from, specific to a label.

Args:
    object: object_type/label data (ex: content, revision, ...)
    end: retrieve the data until this date (timestamp)

Returns:
    The api url to fetch the label's data

methods.name: swh.counters.history.History._adapt_format
documentation:
Javascript expects timestamps to be in milliseconds
and counter values as floats

Args
    item: List of 2 elements, timestamp and counter

Return:
    Normalized tuple (timestamp in js expected time, counter as float)

methods.name: swh.counters.history.History._get_timestamp_history
documentation: Return the live values of an object
Manage the historical data of the counters

documentation:
Add the provided keys to the collection
Only count new keys.

methods.name: swh.counters.interface.CountersInterface.get_count

methods.name: swh.counters.interface.CountersInterface.get_counts

methods.name: swh.counters.interface.HistoryInterface.get_history
documentation:
Return the content of an history file previously created
by the refresh_counters method

methods.name: swh.counters.interface.HistoryInterface.refresh_history
documentation:
Refresh the cache file containing the counters historical data.
It can be an aggregate of live data and static data stored on
a separate file

functions.name: swh.counters.journal_client.process_journal_messages
documentation:
Count the number of different values of an object's property.
It allow for example to count the persons inside the
Release (authors) and Revision (authors and committers) classes

functions.name: swh.counters.journal_client.process_revisions
documentation:
Count the number of different authors and committers on the
revisions (in the person collection)

functions.name: swh.counters.journal_client.process_releases
documentation:
Count the number of different authors on the
releases (in the person collection)

License: GNU General Public License version 3, or any later version
See top-level LICENSE file for more information

classes.name: swh.counters.kafka_client.KeyOrientedJournalClient
documentation:
Journal Client implementation which only decodes the message keys.
This does not need to bother with the message deserialization (contrary
to {class}`swh.journal.client.JournalClient`)
Message values are still passed unparsed to `worker_fn` so it can
deserialize and use it if needed.
